title: Cytisus benehoavensis
taxonomy: Cytisus benehoavensis is classified as a species within the genus Cytisus, which belongs to the family Fabaceae. This plant is a type of broom, specifically a species of broom tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The genus Cytisus is further classified within the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide range of legume plants. As part of the Fabaceae family, Cytisus benehoavensis is closely related to other plants like beans, peas, and lentils. The classification of Cytisus benehoavensis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Fabaceae family.

intro:
There is no information available on a plant called Cytisus benehoavensis. However, I can provide information on Cytisus, a genus of flowering plants.

Cytisus is a genus of flowering plants in the legume family, commonly known as broom. Cytisus species, such as Cytisus scoparius (broom), have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their health benefits. Some of the bioactive constituents found in Cytisus species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. The most common herbal preparations made with Cytisus species are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, used to treat various health conditions such as inflammation, fever, and digestive issues.
